hooks.prompt:
You are updating the technical specification at /Users/masubi/dev/jobs-agent/docs/spec.md when requirements change.

STEP 1: Run `git -C /Users/masubi/dev/jobs-agent log --oneline -20` to see recent commits, then `git -C /Users/masubi/dev/jobs-agent diff HEAD~5 HEAD` to see recent changes.

STEP 2: Identify whether any of the recent changes represent a REQUIREMENT CHANGE. A requirement change is:
  - A new feature or user-facing capability
  - A changed behavior or constraint (e.g., word limits, page limits, retention windows)
  - A new API endpoint, request/response field, or data model change
  - A new integration, external dependency, or service
  - A changed algorithm or business rule (matching scores, thresholds, priorities)
  - A new security/validation rule

DO NOT treat these as requirement changes:
  - Bug fixes that restore intended behavior
  - Refactoring, renaming, or code style changes
  - Test additions or test fixes
  - Dependency version bumps (unless the upgrade changes behavior)
  - Documentation edits
  - Log message changes
  - Performance tuning (unless it changes a documented SLA)

STEP 3: If there are NO requirement changes, exit silently without touching spec.md.

STEP 4: If there ARE requirement changes, read /Users/masubi/dev/jobs-agent/docs/spec.md. It is a technical specification with numbered sections (1. System Overview, 2. Tech Stack, 3. Data Storage, 4. Job Management, 5. Job Matching, 6. Document Generation, 7. Profile Management, 8. Pipeline Scheduler, 9. LLM Queue, 10. Auth & Security, 11. Frontend, 12. Known Limitations). Use the Edit tool to update the relevant section(s) with the new requirement. Preserve the existing format — narrative technical prose and tables, NOT a checklist.

STEP 5: Report what you updated (or that no changes were needed) in under 3 sentences.
